name: Rapa cMRI older-adult substudy is completed with no posted results
summary: The completed Rapa/cMRI substudy is direct no-results Rapalogs context because it tested low-dose rapamycin in older adults with cardiac functional endpoints but has not posted registry results.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT04742777 as a completed 20-participant phase 2 substudy of 1 mg daily rapamycin for eight weeks in adults aged 70 to 95, with cardiac MRI systolic, diastolic, aortic-area, and aortic-distensibility endpoints and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
time_horizon: Eight weeks of daily rapamycin with cardiac MRI follow-up
quantitative_note: Actual enrollment is 20; actual primary and study completion were 2025-09-09; results were not posted.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of efficacy or harm.
The study is open-label and single-group.
Cardiac MRI endpoints are aging-relevant but do not establish systemic geroprotection without posted results.
